accept: Decision documented on whether bundle_strategy is extended, supplemented, or left as-is
If a new strategy mode is added, its semantics are documented in CLAUDE.md alongside the existing modes
The orchestration-design.md log-noise section is updated with the actual mechanism rather than the abstract framing it has today
payload.description:
The existing `bundle_strategy=per_session` setting brackets a single worker's session — claim+work+close get bundled into the work commit on close. In Mode B the worker's session is short-lived (one issue) and the orchestrator's session is long-lived (many workers, many cycles). The orchestrator-batched-claims pattern described in the log-noise section of orchestration-design.md implicitly assumes orchestrator-scoped bundling that doesn't exist today; "per_session" is ambiguous as written about whose session is bracketed.

Options:
(a) Extend bundle_strategy with a new mode (e.g. `per_orchestrator_cycle`) whose semantics bracket the orchestrator's dispatch cycle, not the worker's session.
(b) Leave bundle_strategy as-is (worker-scoped) and have the orchestrator handle multi-op writes via a future batch surface (act-batch / act-create-multi etc., related to the multi-op atomicity/batching open question).
(c) Replace per_session with a more general policy expression that captures both worker- and orchestrator-scoped behavior.

The choice has implications for the v0 orchestrator implementation (CC with sub-agents): if the orchestrator just calls act N times in sequence and accepts N commits, the question is deferred; if orchestrator-scoped bundling is the answer, the implementation needs the new strategy mode before noise reduction becomes real.

Surfaced in the act-334e review (abstractions reviewer, ~85% confidence — noted that per_session semantics are worker-scoped today but the orchestrator-batched-claims pattern needs an orchestrator-scoped equivalent).
payload.title: bundle_strategy needs an orchestrator-scoped equivalent for Mode B
